articles.author: The Associated Press
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel killed Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in strikes on Saturday. President Donald Trump called on Iranians to “take back” their country.
articles.title: Live updates: Iran's future unclear after Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in US-Israeli attack

articles.author: Bridget Brown, Lorian Belanger, Ricardo Benassi, Hrvoje Hranjski
articles.description: After Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in military strikes by the U.S. and Israel, the nation's foreign minister announced a search for a new supreme leader.
articles.title: Live updates: Iranian president says new leadership council ‘has begun its work’ after death of supreme leader

articles.author: Adil Jawad, Munir Ahmed
articles.description: Authorities say at least nine people were killed in clashes with police on Sunday after hundreds of protesters stormed the U.S. Consulate in the Pakistani port city of Karachi. The violence came after the United States and Israel attacked Iran and killed the Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Police said the protesters have been dispersed, and the situation is under control. Karachi is the largest city in Pakistan and the protesters were minority Shiites in the predominantly Sunni Muslim country. Other protests were planned in cities across the country.
articles.title: At least 9 killed as Shiites storm US Consulate in Pakistan over killing of Iran's supreme leader

articles.author: Brian Melley, Angela Charlton
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel have launched major attacks on Iran, whose state media say Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead. Iran counterattacked, and exchanges of fire continued Sunday. U.S. President Donald Trump said the attacks aim to crush Iran’s military and stop the production of a nuclear weapon. Iran fired drones and missiles at Israel and aimed strikes at U.S. military installations in Bahrain, Kuwait and Qatar. Several countries closed their airspace. Iranian state media cited the Red Crescent as saying that at least 201 people have been killed. An Israeli emergency service reported one death there.
articles.title: US and Israel launch major attack on Iran amid nuclear tensions

articles.author: Cara Rubinsky, Marc Levy, Josh Funk
articles.description: The attack on Iran by the U.S. and Israel has disrupted flights across the Middle East and beyond. Countries around the region closed their airspace. Emirates Airlines has suspended all flights to and from Dubai until at least Sunday afternoon. The Qatar airport has closed until at least Monday morning. Israeli airspace also remains closed Sunday. Hundreds of thousands of travelers were either stranded or diverted to other airports after Israel, Qatar, Syria, Iran, Iraq, Kuwait and Bahrain closed their airspaces. More than 1,800 flights by major Middle Eastern airlines have been canceled.
articles.title: Attack on Iran closes Middle East airports and strands travelers

articles.description: Pezeshkian made the comment in a prerecorded message aired on Iranian state television. He is one of three officials on the council.
articles.title: Iranian president says new leadership council ‘has begun its work’ after death of supreme leader

articles.description: Thousands of Iranian government supporters filled the main square in the central city of Yazd in mourning on Sunday, led by Shiite clerics, after US and Israeli strikes killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
articles.title: Thousands of Iranian government supporters mourning Khamenei chant 'Death to America'

articles.description: Israel’s rescue services Magen David Adom said Saturday night that a woman in the Tel Aviv area had died after being injured in an Iranian missile attack.
articles.title: AP is There: Iranian missile hits area of Tel Aviv and Israeli authorities respond
